commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No narrow automated repair is indicated; maintainers need to accept or reject live MCP materialization from an operator.read inventory request.; Needs attention: No credential handling or tool-execution bug was found, but the PR intentionally broadens a read-scoped gateway method into MCP transport startup/listing. (sha=297a4a9e090258b2d0308d398a8558a4363efe22)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `tools.effective` materialize policy-filtered bundled MCP tools into an `mcp` source group and updates the protocol, docs, UI labels, i18n output, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's `tools.effective` handler uses only the static inventory resolver while the embedded run path materializes MCP tools later. I did not run the harness in this read-only review, but the PR includes before/after Gateway RPC proof with a real stdio MCP server.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automated repair is indicated; maintainers need to accept or reject live MCP materialization from an `operator.read` inventory request.

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.effective` / the Agent Tools dashboard only reported the static OpenClaw tool inventory, so bundled MCP tools could be live at runtime but absent from the session-effective inventory.
- Solution: keep the static resolver side-effect-free, and add a session-scoped `tools.effective` materialization path that appends policy-filtered bundled MCP tools as an `mcp` source group.
- What changed: effective inventory projection now supports `source: "mcp"`; `tools.effective` materializes session MCP tools through the existing runtime/materializer/policy path; cache invalidates on MCP config fingerprint changes; server-derived `spawnedBy` is threaded into final policy; docs/protocol/UI/i18n now cover MCP effective entries.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider transport changes, no Tool Search/Code Mode surface changes, no auto-reply `/tools` command changes, no install-package-dir changes, no broad shared-runtime refactor, and no claim that the reporter-specific SSE runtime no-`tools/list` path is fixed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A (intentionally not closing #76724; reporter-specific SSE runtime path was not reproduced)
- Related #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list the MCP tools. Current runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but `tools.effective` used a separate static inventory path and never touched the session MCP runtime. That made the dashboard misleading even when the runtime path was able to use MCP tools.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` omitted bundled MCP server tools from the session-effective inventory even when a real MCP server was configured.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw gateway from this branch at `297a4a9e090258b2d0308d398a8558a4363efe22`, QA mock OpenAI provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/root/repos/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-rpc-proof-after-rebase-297a4a9e09-20260519T184347+0800-summary.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-rpc-proof-after.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from the harness; summary file SHA-256 `3036a0709da74f7e9842ad6d6b8ed490a6df1580e808e355bafb5c51ffe777f3`:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "upstreamCommit": "297a4a9e090258b2d0308d398a8558a4363efe22",
  "effective": {
    "profile": "coding",
    "groupCount": 3,
    "toolCount": 21,
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the actual Gateway WebSocket RPC `tools.effective` returns an `mcp` group containing `reproProbe__probe_tool` after creating a session with a real configured stdio MCP server.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; the previous current-main SSE harness did not reproduce the original no-`tools/list` runtime claim, so this PR is intentionally scoped to the dashboard/session-effective inventory layer. #80909 raw provider-body behavior is also out of scope.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch proof `/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-rpc-proof-summary.json` on current main returned 20 tools and omitted `reproProbe__probe_tool` from `tools.effective` while the runtime provider path could include it.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `tools.effective` called the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` resolver, which builds from `createOpenClawCodingTools()` only. Bundled MCP tools are appended later in the model-run path through `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime()` and `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun()`, so the dashboard inventory and runtime provider path diverged.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered core/plugin/channel grouping but did not cover bundled MCP materialization through the `tools.effective` handler/RPC path.
- Contributing context (if known): related PR #70876 also targets MCP tools in inventory, but mixes auto-reply `/tools` and install-package-dir changes and does not patch the gateway `tools.effective` path directly.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `tools.effective` materializes bundled MCP tools into an `mcp` group and omits them when final effective tool policy filters them out; the cache invalidates when only the MCP config fingerprint changes; static inventory projection treats `bundle-mcp` separately from generic plugin tools; UI labels effective MCP chips.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is at the seam between the static inventory resolver and the gateway `tools.effective` handler, not at provider transport.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none found for bundled MCP in `tools-effective` / `tools-effective-inventory` before this patch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Agent Tools "Available Right Now" / `tools.effective` inventory can now show live bundled MCP tools for the current session as MCP-sourced tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[tools.effective] -> [static createOpenClawCodingTools inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

After:
[tools.effective] -> [static inventory]
                  -> [session MCP runtime materialize]
                  -> [final bundled-tool policy]
                  -> [core/plugin/channel + mcp]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `tools.effective` can now materialize configured session MCP servers to list their tools. It uses the existing session MCP runtime/materializer, server-derived session metadata, and final effective tool policy; it releases the materializer lease and leaves runtime lifecycle to the existing session runtime manager. It does not execute MCP tools. Depending on MCP transport, listing can start a stdio server process or connect to a configured remote MCP endpoint, matching the existing runtime materialization path.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev environment
- Runtime/container: local Node via `node --import tsx`
- Model/provider: QA mock OpenAI provider, `mock-openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket RPC `sessions.create` then `tools.effective`
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile: "coding"`, `tools.toolSearch: false`, `tools.codeMode.enabled: false`, `mcp.servers.reproProbe` stdio server command `node <tmp>/dummy-mcp-server.mjs`

### Steps

1. Start QA mock provider and local Gateway from this branch.
2. Create a session with `sessions.create`.
3. Call `tools.effective` for that session.
4. Parse returned groups/tools and assert `reproProbe__probe_tool` appears in an `mcp` source group.

### Expected

- `tools.effective` includes the materialized MCP tool in an MCP group.

### Actual

- `containsMcpTarget: true`; `groupSummaries` includes `{ "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
  - direct i18n check (`node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check`)
  - direct protocol generation + generated diff check
  - direct tsgo core
  - direct tsgo core-test
  - direct tsgo test-ui
  - targeted root vitest: 4 files / 54 tests
  - targeted UI chromium browser vitest: 1 file / 6 tests
  - real Gateway RPC proof with real stdio MCP server includes `reproProbe__probe_tool` in `tools.effective`
- Edge cases checked:
  - final effective policy can filter materialized MCP tools out
  - cache invalidates when only the MCP config fingerprint changes
  - `spawnedBy` from the server session entry is passed into final effective tool policy
  - static resolver still groups `bundle-mcp` separately without runtime materialization
  - UI labels MCP effective chips through i18n
  - generated locale files only add `agentTools.mcp` plus i18n metadata/cache entries
- What you did **not** verify:
  - reporter's exact Flask SSE MCP server / v2026.5.2 setup
  - Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior (out of scope; previous investigation showed different expected surfaces)
  - live sender identity parity for operator RPCs; the PR intentionally uses server-derived session projection only

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: dashboard inventory can now start a configured MCP server to list tools.
  - Mitigation: limited to session-scoped `tools.effective`, uses existing MCP runtime lifecycle, releases materializer lease, and does not execute tools.
- Risk: dashboard/policy projection may still differ from a live inbound-message run when the UI lacks sender identity.
  - Mitigation: do not accept caller-supplied auth/delivery context; use only server-derived session fields such as provider, group, owner scope, and `spawnedBy`; document the result as a server-derived projection.
- Risk: generated i18n metadata churn makes the PR look larger than the semantic UI change.
  - Mitigation: `pnpm ui:i18n:sync` generated the locale/cache updates; post-rebase direct i18n check passed; focused diff inspection confirms only `agentTools.mcp` content was added.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.
target.author: nxmxbbd

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
